title: Example Defended Bid Launchpad

summary.thesis: A defended bid that keeps replenishing at the same price, while aggressive buyers continue to hit, can become a launchpad toward higher liquidity.
summary.operator_translation: Only look for continuation if the defended price keeps holding and the market is still capable of re-accelerating upward.
summary.ai_usage_note: Prioritize this card when replay events show repeated same-price defense plus upward initiative and an obvious liquidity target above.

when_relevant: There is a defended price that has already absorbed multiple tests.
Same-price replenishment is observed at or near the defended bid.
Aggressive buyers continue to print while price stops making lower lows.
There is visible upper liquidity or a clear nearby target.
required_evidence: same_price_replenishment count is meaningfully above baseline
significant_liquidity track remains active near price
initiative drive or net delta shifts in the direction of the defended side
price does not continue lower despite repeated sell pressure
confirmation_signals: Higher low or reclaimed local pivot after the defended price holds
Aggressive buying expands while best bid remains firm
Price begins to travel toward the upper focus region
invalidation_signals: The defended price fails without renewed bid support
Replenishment stops while price remains heavy
Aggressive selling resumes and price cannot reclaim the defended area
no_trade_conditions: Do not open in the middle of balance without a defended price at the edge.
Do not open just because a large bid exists if there is no same-price replenishment.
Do not open if the larger-session context is pushing into a stronger opposing region above.

entry_archetypes.name: first reclaimed higher low
entry_archetypes.description: Entry after the defended bid survives and price reclaims the most recent micro pivot.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the reclaim occurs directly into heavy overhead pressure without follow-through.

entry_archetypes.name: pullback into defended price
entry_archetypes.description: Entry on a pullback that retests the defended price and shows renewed buyer aggression.
entry_archetypes.risk_note: Avoid if the pullback becomes a deep acceptance below the defended level.

management_notes: Expect shallow pullbacks if the launchpad is real.
Watch whether upper liquidity is consumed cleanly or only tapped and rejected.
If the first target is harvested, switch attention to post-harvest balance or reversal behavior.
review_questions: Was the defended price actually defended multiple times, or was it only a single visible bid?
Did the entry align with the larger session and local trend context?
Was the operator too early before initiative actually returned?
Did price reach upper liquidity, and what happened immediately after harvest?
